message:
fix(agentmail): improve message lookup and enforce strict loading

Two related improvements to agent mail handling:

internal/agentmail/unified.go - Deeper inbox search:
- Problem: UnifiedMessenger.Read() only searched last 100 messages,
  causing "message not found" errors for older message IDs.
- Solution: If message not found in initial fetch, retry with limit=1000.
- Uses helper function findMsg() to avoid code duplication.
- Improves error message to "not available or not configured".
- Trade-off: Slightly slower for missing messages (two API calls).

internal/agentmail/*_test.go - Strict loading behavior:
- LoadSessionAgent with empty projectKey used to scan subdirectories
  looking for any matching agent file. This was ambiguous and could
  return unexpected agents.
- New behavior: Empty projectKey only checks legacy path, returns nil
  if not found. No subdirectory scanning.
- Tests updated to expect nil instead of "found some agent somewhere".
- Prevents silent misattribution in multi-project scenarios.

Impact:
- Agents must use explicit projectKey for reliable loading.
- Old sessions without projectKey gracefully return nil.
- Message reads are more robust for older messages.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
